description:
## Status: deferred after investigation (2026-05-18)

The provenance refactor (EmitterSite + produces + holders, unified
worklist absorbing MakeClosure via live opaque_arities, reachability
via the holders graph) is the right structural answer to the issues
named in fz-rh5's epic prose. The typer-level rewrite was implemented
and all 54 \`ir_typer::tests\` passed against it.

**The blocker**: two downstream codegen tests
(\`sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion\`,
\`map_higher_order_renders_doubled_list\`) fail because the new spec
set is *correct but different*. Specifically:

  - \`inline_single_use_conts\` (in \`ir_inline.rs\`) mutates
    \`ModuleTypes\` in place after the final typer call, assuming
    structural invariants about which (cont_fn_id, key) pairs
    appear together. The provenance refactor's spec set —
    accumulating *all* live emit edges including narrow cont specs
    that the old reachability sweep would have pruned — breaks
    those assumptions. Result: after the inliner mutates
    \`module_types\`, only the widened cont spec survives, and
    codegen's narrow lookup (via \`cont_input_key\`) misses.

  - The interaction is not a typer bug; it's that
    \`inline_single_use_conts\` was co-designed with the old
    typer's specific over-/under-specialization patterns. Making
    PROV.6 land cleanly requires auditing every consumer of
    \`ModuleTypes\` that mutates it post-hoc.

**Decision**: keep this ticket open as a known-good design that
needs broader scope. Don't ship a partial implementation — that's
exactly the "layering slop on top of slop" pattern fz-rh5 was
created to avoid.

**Re-scope as**: \`fz-PROV.6.v2\` (TBD) — provenance refactor +
co-design with \`inline_single_use_conts\` and any other in-place
\`ModuleTypes\` mutators. Will require its own DAG.

## Original design (preserved for the re-scope)

title: fz-PROV.6: provenance-based spec lifetime — EmitterSite + holders + unified worklist
